test_cases.description: Housing federation conference address — CEO, 8 minutes, government accountability argument, three embedded key messages
test_cases.input_summary: James Aldridge, National Housing Federation CEO; annual conference; 600 professionals; 8 minutes; three key messages about planning failures, sector performance, 10-year plan endorsement ask; authoritative not combative register
test_cases.notes: Does not open with thanks. Three key messages embedded at natural argument pivots, not listed. Three-question sequence built for pause and effect. Call to action at close is specific and time-bounded ('by end of this month'). Sentences average 18 words — appropriate for spoken delivery. Delivery note at top adds practical value without cluttering the script.

test_cases.description: Product launch speech — CMO, 5-minute stage address, technology conference, two key messages, teleprompter delivery
test_cases.input_summary: CMO of a SaaS company; product launch at a technology conference; 5 minutes; two key messages: product solves a specific workflow bottleneck; company is expanding to European market; teleprompter delivery; direct and confident register
test_cases.notes: Teleprompter format correctly applied — slightly shorter lines, predictable rhythm. Relevance note: included a third implicit message about competitive differentiation that was not in the brief — could cause problems if the company has cleared messaging only for the two stated points. Fluency note: opening sentence in the product demo section had a relative clause that would be awkward on teleprompter. Consistency: no fabricated product features.

test_cases.description: Award acceptance speech — individual receiving lifetime achievement award at media industry dinner, 3 minutes, personal reflection expected
test_cases.input_summary: Veteran documentary filmmaker receiving a lifetime achievement award; 3-minute acceptance speech at a media industry dinner; personal reflection on career expected; no key messages per se; warm and humble register; anecdote about a specific formative assignment to include
test_cases.notes: Correctly shifted from the structured argumentative format to a more reflective, personal register. The requested anecdote was naturalistically integrated, not inserted. Relevance note: output included a forward-looking paragraph about the future of documentary filmmaking — not requested in the brief, and slightly out of place in an acceptance speech which should close on gratitude or personal reflection rather than a keynote-style position statement. Actionability: the optional delivery note was included and appropriate.
